suggested_meta: Mirau WLI objective lenses for precise 3D surface measurements. Achieve high-resolution, repeatable metrology data. Explore specs and configurations.
content_recommendations: ['Add a dedicated section explaining how white light interferometry works, including a diagram of the Mirau interferometer optical path — users searching this term often need foundational understanding before evaluating objectives', 'Include a comparison table showing key differences between available Mirau objective magnifications (e.g., 5x, 10x, 20x, 50x) with lateral resolution, field of view, working distance, and recommended surface types for each', 'Add application examples with images showing specific surface measurement results (e.g., semiconductor wafer roughness, machined metal surface topography, thin-film step height) to help users match objectives to their use case', 'Create a section addressing common selection criteria: when to choose WLI over confocal or phase-shifting interferometry objectives, including trade-offs in vertical resolution, speed, and surface compatibility', "Add FAQ schema markup covering questions like 'What is white light interferometry used for?', 'What is a Mirau objective?', and 'What surfaces can WLI measure?' to capture featured snippet and People Also Ask placements"]
